goal: README 只保留一个 Hosted Hub 入口

summary.title: Simplify README Hosted Hub link
summary.what: Changed README.md and README.zh.md to expose https://mainline.sh/hub/ as the single Hosted Hub entry and removed the GitHub Pages snapshot URL from the README-facing Hub section.
summary.why: The public docs should not ask users to choose between the website Hub and the GitHub Pages snapshot when the product needs one clear Hub entry.
summary.user_goal: README 只保留一个 Hosted Hub 入口

decisions.chose: Use https://mainline.sh/hub/ as the Hosted Hub link in both English and Chinese READMEs.
decisions.rationale: The website Hub is the canonical user-facing product URL.

decisions.chose: Remove GitHub Pages URL/details from the README and leave static publishing details to the reference docs.
decisions.rationale: GitHub Pages is an implementation/deployment detail; putting it in README recreated the two-entry confusion.

rejected.alternative: Keep both Hub links and rename one as a backup snapshot.
rejected.reason: The user agreed that one Hub entry is enough.

rejected.alternative: Remove all static publishing references from docs.
rejected.reason: The GitHub Pages workflow remains useful operational detail in reference docs; the problem was the public README entry.

review_notes: Ran git diff --check and git diff --cached --check.
Confirmed README.md and README.zh.md only show https://mainline.sh/hub/ for Hosted Hub.

architectural_claims: README should expose one canonical hosted Hub URL: https://mainline.sh/hub/.
GitHub Pages publishing remains reference-level operational detail, not README-level product navigation.
behavioral_changes: README readers now see one Hosted Hub entry instead of the GitHub Pages snapshot URL.
security_implications: No runtime or security behavior changed; documentation-only update.
